---
title: "Systematic Review Protocol"
subtitle: "PROSPERO Registration Draft"
author:
  - name: "[Lead Author, Credentials]"
    affiliation:
      - name: "[Institution / Organization]"
        city: "[City]"
        region: "[State / Province]"
        country: "[Country]"
        url: "[Organization URL]"
    email: "[author@example.org]"
    orcid: "[0000-0000-0000-0000]"
    url: "[Profile URL]"
    corresponding: true
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
format:
  docx:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl  # Default — agents replace with the project's configured style
---

<!-- If project-config.yaml defines research_assistant.authors, replace the placeholders in this front matter with those author records before rendering. -->
<!-- CSL style resolution: project output_defaults.csl → user default_citation_style → apa.csl fallback. Agents substitute the correct style when instantiating this template. -->
<!-- If RWA is cited in this document, add the `vanzyl2026rwa` entry from templates/rwa-citation.bib to references.bib. -->
<!-- CITATION FORMAT: All citations MUST use Pandoc citekeys (@key or [@key]), never plain-text author-year. Every cited work needs a BibTeX entry in references.bib. -->

# Registration Information

- **Registration platform:** PROSPERO / OSF / Other
- **Registration number:** [Pending / CRD...]
- **Date of registration:**

# Review Question

## Research Question

[State the primary research question clearly]

## Question Framework

- **Framework used:** PICO / PEO / SPIDER / PCC
- **Population:**
- **Intervention/Exposure:**
- **Comparator:**
- **Outcome:**

# Background and Rationale

[Describe the rationale for the review, existing evidence gaps, and why this review is needed]

# Objectives

[State specific objectives of the review]

# Eligibility Criteria

## Inclusion Criteria

| Criterion | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Population | |
| Intervention/Exposure | |
| Comparator | |
| Outcome | |
| Study design | |
| Time period | |
| Language | |

## Exclusion Criteria

- [List exclusion criteria]

# Information Sources

## Electronic Databases

| Database | Platform | Date searched |
|----------|----------|---------------|
| PubMed/MEDLINE | NCBI | |
| OpenAlex | API | |
| Semantic Scholar | API | |
| Europe PMC | EBI | |
| Scopus | Elsevier | |
| Web of Science | Clarivate | |
| [Others] | | |

## Other Sources

- Reference lists of included studies
- Grey literature sources
- Contact with experts
- Trial registries

# Search Strategy

## Concept Decomposition

| Concept | MeSH Terms | Free-text Synonyms |
|---------|-----------|-------------------|
| Population | | |
| Intervention/Exposure | | |
| Outcome | | |

## Boolean Query Structure

```
(Population terms) AND (Intervention terms) AND (Outcome terms)
```

## Full Search Strategy

[Insert database-specific search strategies here, generated with the @systematic-reviewer agent]

# Study Selection

## Screening Process

1. **Deduplication:** [Method]
2. **Title/abstract screening:** [Number of reviewers, independence, tool used]
3. **Full-text screening:** [Number of reviewers, independence]
4. **Disagreement resolution:** [Method]

# Data Extraction

## Data Items

| Variable | Description | Source |
|----------|-------------|--------|
| | | |

## Data Extraction Form

[Describe or attach the data extraction form]

# Risk of Bias Assessment

- **Tool:** RoB 2.0 / ROBINS-I / NOS / CASP / JBI / Other
- **Domains assessed:**
- **Number of assessors:**
- **Disagreement resolution:**

# Data Synthesis

## Qualitative Synthesis

[Describe the approach to narrative synthesis]

## Quantitative Synthesis (if applicable)

- **Software:** R (metafor) / Python (statsmodels) / Other
- **Effect measure:**
- **Model:** Fixed-effects / Random-effects
- **Heterogeneity assessment:** I-squared, Q statistic, tau-squared
- **Subgroup analyses planned:**
- **Sensitivity analyses planned:**

# Confidence in Cumulative Evidence

- **Framework:** GRADE / Other
- **Domains assessed:**

# Use of Research Workflow Assistant (RWA)

This protocol may have been prepared with assistance from Research Workflow Assistant (RWA), accessed through GitHub Copilot in VS Code:

- **Use:** [Describe specific use: search strategy development, template generation, workflow planning, or drafting support]
- **Human oversight:** All decisions regarding the research question, eligibility criteria, methods, and final approval were made by the research team
- **Verification:** Cite RWA as [@vanzyl2026rwa] when it is described here, and verify any cited standards, database attributions, or tool references in references.bib before submission
- **ICMJE compliance:** AI tools are not listed as authors; use is disclosed per ICMJE Section II.A.4

# Acknowledgments And AI Disclosure

[Acknowledge non-author contributors or registration support if applicable.]

**RWA disclosure:** Research Workflow Assistant (RWA), accessed through GitHub Copilot in VS Code, may have been used to assist with [specific protocol-development tasks]. The authors reviewed, revised, and verified all AI-assisted content and take full responsibility for the final protocol.

If you mention RWA in the acknowledgments, cite it as [@vanzyl2026rwa].

# Timeline

| Phase | Target Start | Target End |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| Protocol registration | | |
| Search execution | | |
| Screening | | |
| Data extraction | | |
| Risk of bias assessment | | |
| Data synthesis | | |
| Manuscript preparation | | |
| Submission | | |

# Amendments

Any amendments to this protocol will be documented with date, description, and rationale.

| Date | Section | Amendment | Rationale |
|------|---------|-----------|-----------|
| | | | |

# References
